Others escaped south to Bloodtide Coast. You'll find their encampment along Stormbluff Isle's northern shore.

Otho's voyage to retrieve the keystone was successful. At least, what he was able to salvage of it.    While Manikaz's efforts to open the gate and secure our escape from this prison have been fine, we now look to the keystone for answers... Hope, albeit subtle, builds in the city.
Ouch... I just want to go home.

Our anti-addiction system discourages excessive play by minors. After 3 hours of play, your rewards are reduced by half; after 5 hours, they are removed completely. You will need to take a break for a full 5 hours before your rewards are reset to their normal rates.
Our employer turns out to be Balthazar. He's ordered us to round up and cage any remaining White Mantle in the camp. This should cull any resistance arising from this revelation.
Our facility that desalinates the brackish waters of the Elon for drinking and irrigation is a marvel to see.
Our gates are being destroyed. Repair them!
Our last ambush went well, but this is no time to rest on our laurels. Tonight, we'll discuss our next phase of the attack. Be on time for once.

Our losses at Cragstead were brutal. We managed to rescue some of the homesteaders, but a few of them were taken prisoner, including Ottilia, an ex-fling of Braham's. We don't know where they were taken yet, but finding their base is next on our list, and the Vigil is hot on their trail.I didn't know Eir had a kid, let alone one as...hardheaded as Braham. His head may be full of steam, but he's got guts if he's willing to stand up to the Master of the Lodge. Reinforcements or not, he ran right into Cragstead, shield raised.
Our next survey runs through the very center of the barrens. A group of our allies is conducting research on the creatures of the area and the effects of Mistburn.

Our priesthood is grateful to you, %str1%. Kormir herself knows of your selflessness on behalf of her temple and her devoted followers.With Balthazar's forces so close, I fear our troubles will continue. If you can find it in your heart to return and help us again, we would welcome your continued defense of the innocents we shelter here.—Priest Hakim

Our records indicate that you acquired a Wings of Love glider skin from us at some point in the past. We recently released a matching backpack skin and would like to offer you one at no additional cost to help complete your set. To pick up your free Wings of Love Backpack Skin, please visit any Black Lion Exchange Specialist and look for it under the Gliders tab. Enjoy!—Black Lion Trading Company

Our sentries bibble-babble always the story of you coming to our cave. One say you carry fifty wounded skritt tied to your head as you jumped over lava. One say you break a fire-troll over your knee and used its pebbles for gunpowder. I know not if these deeds are real, but to us, they are true. Not the same, real and true. True you feel inside. Always follow what is true.
—Sentry-skritt Bordekka

Our signature Wavehawk cocktail offers a sophisticated blend of flavors for the discerning Castoran. You're greeted with aromas of driftwood and bewilderflower. The palette matches the nose but adds a distinct, complex note of vanilla, jungle floor, and a hint of gunpowder residue. Medium bodied, with a dry, sea-spray finish. Limit 2 per customer.    Ingredients:  - Peated Whiskey- Bitters- Citrus  - Bourbon
Our team has powered the Skysplitter!

Our world is constantly in motion, be it the tides of time, the tides of war, or the tides of the sea that swell and churn, covering kingdoms lost and revealing the lands of old.Decimus the Historian takes up his pen once more in this stunning essay on the events that have shaped our world, both figuratively and quite literally. As the guilds expanded and cultural revolutions pushed the races of Tyria closer together, our world shifted to one of modernity and prosperity.But as the Elder Dragons woke and unnatural cataclysms tore open the flesh of the world, the face of Tyria was forever altered, and new conflicts threaten it in ways heretofore unseen. Humanity is in decline, the balance of power shifting. Only through careful examination of the past can we hope to change our future.
Out of sight and out of mind, beneath the Inquest Command Complex.
Out of sight within the place where sight once stared into space.

Out of the blue, an elusive benefactor named "E" wrote and directed me to the effigy ceremony at Dragon Bash, claiming that the Captain's Council was under some sort of threat. It was a faceless lead, but the only one I had. I made my way to Lion's Arch to see for myself. The ceremony started without a hitch, but then the effigies were lit...As the cultural representatives approached with their offerings, one of the dragon effigies erupted with arcane energies, emitting an electrical current that struck the captains on stage. Inspector Ellen Kiel immediately took control of the scene. With the help of her Lionguard, Kiel and I escorted the wounded to Fort Marriner. Along the way, we were harassed by a group of scrapyard pirates.We delivered the captains to the medical ward, but their wounds were critical. Kiel implored me to return to the festivities, but I'm not in the mood for a celebration anymore. Between this and the Molten Alliance... Something is brewing, and I'm fast growing tired of these constant surprises.

Outrider Arina,  Your father asked me to give you additional details as the leader of this mission, as it is not simply a matter of containing the threat, as you have so often done. In this circumstance, there is evidence to suggest that our enemy has targeted a powerful individual in Tyria.  You have read previous reports on this person and their pivotal role in recent events surrounding the ending of the dragon cycle. In fact, on two prior occasions, your father and myself both petitioned the Wizard's Court to recruit them into the Astral Ward, but we were denied. With the boldness with which our enemy has stepped up their attacks, it is not only likely but probable that you may encounter this individual on your mission. If this happens, do not interfere or even interact with them.  They are to be handled with the utmost respect, but it is not your place to involve this individual in our affairs. That may yet change, but for now, maintain the edict.  Remain safe, remain vigilant.  —M

Overseer, I can only offer my apologies—what the director sees in the principal biomancer is beyond me, but there's no doubting the cruelty to which we would be subjected should he become aware of our information-sharing arrangement.    As far as his experiments are concerned, Sebb has been requesting a number of artifacts from the dig sites inland. What he intends with said artifacts, I have been unable to discover. But it seems he has acquired enough of the objects to satisfy whatever hypothesis he intends to test.     Again, I do very much appreciate your discretion and your willingness to indulge our "paranoia" with regard to the unconventional nature of these dead-drop locations.
Owl's followers are bolstered by your help. Good job!

Papa! Given Grandfather's Luxon blood, thought you'd be interested to know the Brotherhood has us digging in a mine with a bit of Luxon gear in it! I found some rusty bits off some old mining equipment. Was in rough shape but could be a nice memento. Maybe show this to Grandfather, see what he says?   We're way out here on some spur of the rail line, down in this hole full of jade... Conditions could be better, and the bosses seem to argue a lot, but could be worse, I guess. Hope to see you soon.
